macro_events.event_summary: ExxonMobil and Chevron reported Q1 2026 earnings declines, citing oil shipment disruptions from an ongoing Iran war.
primary_drivers: Middle East shipping/logistics disruption reducing realized volumes for integrated majors
Higher crude price volatility benefiting non-disrupted producers and tanker rates
Reallocation of capital toward domestic US E&Ps with no Middle East exposure

beneficiaries.rationale: Diamondback Energy is a pure-play Permian Basin producer with zero Middle East exposure; benefits from elevated WTI prices driven by Strait of Hormuz/Iran shipping disruption while peers like XOM/CVX absorb logistics losses.
EOG Resources is US-onshore-only (Eagle Ford, Permian, Bakken); receives full benefit of war-premium crude prices without the tanker/shipping disruption hitting integrated majors' upstream volumes.
Frontline is a leading crude tanker operator; Iran war disruption to normal shipping routes forces longer ton-mile voyages and spikes VLCC/Suezmax day rates — direct revenue tailwind.
International Seaways operates crude and product tankers; Middle East conflict reroutes flows around longer paths, tightening tanker supply and lifting spot rates.
Cenovus is Canadian heavy oil producer with North American pipeline egress only; benefits from elevated crude benchmarks without Persian Gulf shipping exposure.

losers.rationale: Direct subject of the headline — earnings miss attributed to Iran war shipment disruption; ExxonMobil has material upstream exposure in Iraq (West Qurna) and LNG/crude lifting through the Persian Gulf region.

Direct subject — earnings declined on shipment disruptions; Chevron has significant Eastern Mediterranean (Tamar/Leviathan gas) and Kazakhstan (TCO, partly routed via regions affected by conflict) exposure.
Schlumberger derives ~25%+ of revenue from Middle East oilfield services; sustained war disrupts project activity and customer capex timing in the region.
Halliburton has meaningful Middle East/North Africa services exposure; war-driven activity disruption pressures international segment revenue.
Delta Air Lines has high jet fuel sensitivity and is unhedged on fuel; sustained crude price elevation from Iran war erodes margins more than peers.
Southwest Airlines lost its structural fuel hedge advantage; elevated crude from Middle East disruption hits unit costs directly.

headlines: Exxon Mobil and Chevron earnings fall as Iran war disrupts oil shipments
